text.title: Glossary Management (Living Doc) Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real engagements applying glossary-management-living-doc. Each entry pairs symptom + root cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Multiple definitions for the same term across docs because PRDs inline-define instead of linking to glossary.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copies a definition into a PRD instead of linking.
antipattern.fix: Open a conflict-resolution record; remove inline definitions; PRD linter blocks inline.

antipattern.symptom: Glossary entry exists but is never used.
antipattern.fix: Term-usage audit script flags 0-mention canonical entries; demote or delete after 6 months.

antipattern.symptom: A defined in terms of B, B in terms of A.
antipattern.root-cause: Team uses metaphor instead of concrete language.
antipattern.fix: Parse alias chain; identify primitive; re-anchor cycle.

antipattern.root-cause: Author 'cleans up' the glossary by deleting.
antipattern.fix: Status=deprecated with replaced_by link; usage audit drives cleanup.
